titlestmt.titleproper: Association of Black Motion Pictures and Television Producers (ABMPTP) records
Finding aid for the Association of Black Motion Pictures and Television Producers (ABMPTP) records LSC.2457
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Sage Scanlon-Perez in the Center for Primary Research Training (CFPRT), under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 April 02.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-20 15:39:58 -0700.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers (ABMPTP) records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers

physdesc.extent: 4 letter boxes and 1 legal sized box.

did.abstract:
The Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers (ABMPTP) was formed in Los Angeles in 1980 September 19 as a way to unify the voices of Black producers, navigate a corrupt industry, and in their own words, "to improve the image and well-being of Black America, to speak for the voiceless and those to whom we owe our existence within the purview of membership." Their work happened in tandem with organizations both locally and nationally using different avenues to promote representation, advance equity and empower Black Americans.
This collection consists of administrative records, correspondence, financial records, press, meeting minutes by the governing board, standing committees, and sub-committees. It documents the organizational leadership and operations including elections, membership, collaborations, communications with studios, committee work, and script submission as produced by ABMPTP. Correspondence documents communication with studios, elected officials, individual artists and civil rights organizations, both locally and nationally, including the NAACP and Media Artists Against Discrimination.
Some portions of the collection were not explicitly created by the ABMPTP but overlap in terms of time period and focus, including legal affairs records connected with the LA Human Relations Commission and NAACP records including: local branch communication, financial records, committee reports and notes, complaint forms, flyers, election materials and event information.
The collection also includes community and business proposals, general records consist of labor data, essays and newsletters, community events and flyers. The collection does not document all activities of the ABMPTP in equal depth. Secretary Cassius Weathersby is present throughout the collection, including personal resumes and communication.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to the UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Backstory Books and Yarn, 2025.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Association of Black Motion Pictures and Television Producers (ABMPTP) records (Collection 2457). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Sage Scanlon-Perez, under the supervision of Emily Wittenberg, 2026

Original order was preserved where discernible and original folder titles were retained where present and where they accurately reflected the contents. In some cases, titles were supplied, revised, or standardized for clarity, consistency, and description.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Administrative/Biographical History
bioghist.p: The Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers (ABMPTP) was formed in Los Angeles in 1980 September 19; as a way to unify the voices of Black producers, navigate a corrupt industry, and in their own words, "to improve the image and well-being of Black America, to speak for the voiceless and those to whom we owe our existence within the purview of membership."

The history of cinema within the United States is intimately intertwined with the history of racial oppression and repression of Black, Indigenous and other people of color. Hollywood is embedded in hegemonic systems of power; made visible through a structure that is overwhelmingly white, and maintains whiteness as normative. These practices are reflected in but not limited to: which projects are picked up by studios and supported with capital and by which producers, actors, and stuntspeople are chosen for projects. In response to rampant discrimination against Black producers and filmmakers and within the context of widespread lack of equal opportunity, ABMPTP organized to provide a voice and support system for producers navigating the industry.
Their work happened in tandem with organizations both locally and nationally using different avenues to promote-representation, advance equity, and empower Black Americans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of administrative records, correspondence, financial records, press, meeting minutes by the governing board, standing committees and sub-committees. It documents the organizational leadership and operations including elections, membership, collaborations, communications with studios, committee work and script submission as produced by the Association of Black Motion Pictures and Television Producers (ABMPTP). Correspondence documents communication with studios, elected officials, individual artists, and civil rights organizations, both locally and nationally, including the NAACP and Media Artists Against Discrimination.

Some portions of the collection were not explicitly created by the ABMPTP but overlap in terms of time period and focus, including legal affairs records connected with the LA Human Relations Commission and NAACP records including: local branch communication, financial records, committee reports and notes, complaint forms, flyers, election materials and event information.

The collection also includes community and business proposals, general records consist of labor data, essays and newsletters, community events and flyers. The collection does not document all activities of the ABMPTP in equal depth. Secretary Cassius Weathersby is present throughout the collection, including personal resumes and communication.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
This collection has been arranged in the following series:
Series 1: ABMPTP Administrative Records (1980-1984)
Subseries 1.1: Founding Records (1981)
Subseries 1.2: Standing Committee Records (1980)
Subseries 1.3: Sub-Committee Records (1981-1983)
Series 2: Civil Rights Organizations' Records (1962-1983)
Series 3: Community/Business Proposals (1968-1993)
Series 4: General Records (1964-1991)
Series 5: Legal Affairs' Records (1964-2001)
Subseries 5.1: NAACP Records (1964-2001)

controlaccess.occupation: African American motion picture producers and directors
controlaccess.subject: African Americans in motion pictures.
Motion pictures -- History.
African American neighborhoods -- California -- Los Angeles -- History
controlaccess.corpname: Association of Black Motion Picture and Television Producers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series with sub-series and files arranged alphabetically; contains three sub-series: Founding Records, Standing Committee Records, and Sub-Committee Records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the internal operations of the elected board, sub and standing-committees; including: meeting minutes, internal communication, membership status and responsibilities, routine administrative operations, and election-related records. Materials showcase changing leadership, collaborations with related organizations, and motivating force and vision of the ABMPTP.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipts, ABMPTP 1981 Year End Report, and various other financial documents and memos.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: ABMPTP 1981 Year End Report Discussion Draft is maintained in original order by paperclip and staple. A folder of various financial documents was also maintained in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original folder has the following handwritten onto its cover: "The hardest thing in America for a Black man to become is enlightened" and "The Reason it's hard for a Black man to become enlightened in America is because he thinks it's hard."
Contents include: Declaration of principles, meeting minutes, sub-committee minutes, memos and internal communication, and ABMPTP Press/Publicity Campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some communication with studios, mostly memos for members, between sub-committees, and newly joined producers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sub-series with files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of the ABMPTP Constitution, and some documents with edits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Two editions maintained in original order, one inside an original envelope,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Application for formal incorporation, approval, and draft of articles of incorporation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: One original envelope in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By-laws to the constitution of the ABMPTP, proposed amendments to the constitution and by-laws, and addition to by-laws.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes from the initial meeting to begin the formation of a producers association. Discussion included: the need of the organization, compiling a list of producers, defining membership eligibility. Also includes a binder with a summary of activities conducted by ABMPTP 1980 September-1981 October.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meeting minutes and agendas encompassing the 8th meeting through the 30th meeting. Includes some of the original handwritten notes, typed originals, and copies. Topics and main themes include: the first election of officers, evaluation of the new member application, discussion of prospective members and accepted applicants, and various committee reports. During the 22nd meeting the ABMPTP reviewed how they communicated with Collette Wood at the NAACP that their constitution and by-laws prevent them from taking an advocacy position. Internal communication about how the association should navigate political organizations, and that they "should be aware so as not to let orgs name become diluted with other org's."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in an original file in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes from the 23rd, 26th-31st meetings. Also includes minutes from various standing/sub-committees, a membership roster from 1982, and a draft of a form: Policy Concerning Submission of Ideas/Materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes from the 31st-43rd and 46th-48th meetings. Notes included: goals of doubling membership by 1982 June, mention of the Black Cinema Expo 1982, collaboration with the Baldwin Entertainment Center, and a review of the costs of participation and subsequent note on cancelling. Includes discussion of NAACP boycott of film industry, motion about member attendance and participation at meetings, and a briefing with the LA Human Relations Commission about their upcoming hearing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in an original folder labeled 'A.B.M.P.T.P.' in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes from the 25th-43rd, 46-47, 49-50th meetings. Includes the 1983 official ballot of officers, issue #8 from Media Matters, and communication from the Western States Black Research Center (WSBRC) about an upcoming Black Film Festival.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, some undated documents are included at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1982 calendar of ABMPTP meetings with studios and minutes from a special election and a meeting in 1984.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in an original folder in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes from meetings in 1983 and some from 1984, a copy of the ABMPTP Constitution, coverage by The Medium on "A Human Success Story: Nate Long Makes it Big Despite The Odds," and a membership roster from 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press about the ABMPTP from Variety and The Hollywood Reporter.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence to Weathersby, some of his resumes, press and interviews, photos of Weathersby, and one booklet about the ABMPTP.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence arranged chronologically and two folders in original order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sub-series with files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ABMPTP/Cable Committee Report from 1981, PBS fact sheet, an overview of home video, cable and pay tv prepared by Ardie Ivie, member of the ABMPTP.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some correspondence and memos from the script submission committee, script submissions, blank forms for script submission.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sub-series with files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication from sub-committee to ABMPTP membership regarding the second meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memos about the Black Cinema Expo planned for 1982. Pamphlet includes more information about the month-long film festival, created with help from the Baldwin Hills Entertainment Complex.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder is arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fundraising Committee meeting minutes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents arranged chronologically, undated documents are at the back of the folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with members, membership rosters over the years, handwritten 1983 official ballot for officers for the ABMPTP, draft of membership application, section 9 project fees, and handwritten notes with individuals' contact information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subcommittee consisting of Charles Walker, Eddie Smith, and Nate Long drafted a discussion paper for ABMPTP membership.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder is arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recap of committee meetings for general ABMPTP membership.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly press coverage cut out of Variety, and one from the Hollywood Reporter, including following headlines: "Black Film and TV Producers Organize," "Black Producers Question NLRB," and "Assn. of Black Producers Formed."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents Civil Rights Organizations working with Los Angeles and nationally during the 1980s with a focus on uplifting the voices and experiences of Black Americans, highlighting racial oppression within the entertainment industry, and organizing events at the community level predominantly throughout South Central Los Angeles. Materials include: flyers, demands for production companies, communications, and meeting minutes.

did.unittitle: Black Anti Defamation Coalition Inc. Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains issues #4 and #5 of Media Matters, an educational project of the Black Anti-Defamation Coalition, inc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of folder arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a list of Black writers for potential membership to CORE. Includes various CORE demands and challenges of Paramount, NBC TV, Tandem Productions, ABC TV

did.unittitle: Greater Los Angeles Media Watch Records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents arranged chronologically, aside from one folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication to/from the Greater Los Angeles Media Watch Coalition, meeting minutes and agendas, a viewer monitor form.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purpose and Program of the Hollywood Race Relations Bureau, detailing the problem, proposed solution, and their protests. Volume 1, number 2 of Youth Division News published by the organization's youth division.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Media Artists Against Discrimination statement of purpose and proposal including implementation and evaluation. Some handouts and internal communication and one brochure from the First Annual Windwalker Awards Benefit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication from multiple organizations including: Black Cinema Lab Research Center, National Negro Foundation, and Negro Actors for Action.

did.unittitle: People United to Save Humanity (PUSH) Records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in a folder in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some communication, mostly meeting minutes from the Soul Picnic Committee, PUSH-West Convening Committee, PUSH-West Program/Activities Committee, and PUSH-West Organizational Steering Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes community and business proposals for the entertainment industry. Majority of the proposals are local to Los Angeles and surrounding communities, like Watts and Compton. Throughline between a majority of the proposals is the stated purpose of providing training and education to young men and women from minority and disadvantaged communities for jobs in the technical crafts and skills of the motion picture industry. Documents plans, proposed budgets, market analysis, and community need.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the purpose and status of the Atlanta Cinema Center, the proposal for the "training institution geared primarily to the purposes of teaching and training young men and women from all minority and disadvantaged communities in and around Atlanta." The proposal details the need for the center, program concept, plan of operation, placement, expressions of support, proposed funding, and program evaluation and development.

did.unittitle: Business Plan for CC TV Network

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in a portfolio in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note from the table of contents explains: "this condensed version is intended to give the reader a basic understanding of our business. The first five sections discuss the nature of the business and the business environment in which we will operate. Competitive reasons, we do not wish to distribute the details of our business strategies and plans. Therefore, sections 6 through 16 are discussed in summary form."

did.unittitle: Community Satellite Network: The Community Channel (Proposal)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Stapled in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Community Satellite Network proposal including an overview, characteristics of the Black market, Overview of Cable Programming Industry, Analysis of available Black oriented programming, and proposed programming format.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A proposal for the Compton Cinema Center including purpose and status, need for the center, program concept, objectives, administrative and faculty members, and roll out phases. Includes copies of handwritten notes and drafts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original binding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Culture Cinema Confidential Report including: introduction, staff, locations and financial projection, design, construction, equipment and furnishings, management training, advertising and public relations, accounting and control, stock options and growth potential. And some pamphlets about Culture cinema.

did.unittitle: Kilpatrick Cambridge Theatre Arts School's Records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Two folders maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One folder consisting of incoming letters. The other contains an introduction to the Kilpatrick-Cambridge Theatre Arts School, proposal outline, workshop goals, and various handouts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: One folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of a chart for various levels of organizing, including: Black Community Development training center, phases of employment, Black top talent representation, and more.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Rusty binding was removed for preservation purposes, however the two copies of the proposal are maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of a proposal for special marketing films (a proposed division of a major motion picture studio), including: an introduction, proposal, principal functions, approach, demographic study of the United States Black population, and copies of news clippings.

did.unittitle: Urban Motion Pictures and the Civic Digitek Center Business Plan

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original rusty binding was removed for preservation concerns, however the proposal is maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Business plan by Cassius Weathersby broken down into eleven scenes, including an industry overview, operations, projected effects, marketing, distribution, budget and site plan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Records are maintained in original order within an original folder labeled, 'The Watts Training Center Larry Clements (2nd proposal)'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of a proposal detailing the purpose and status of the Watts Training Center for the Television, Film, Radio, and Recording Industry. Including handwritten notes and edits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in an original folder in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal by William W. Larrabure, Julian L. Combs, and Robert A. Tyndal for a 36-week intensive training course, projected to culminate in 90% employment of participants and/or placement within the audio-visual industry.

did.unittitle: Youth Development Center/Motion Picture Studio Incubator

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in a binder in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for a youth development center/motion picture studio incubator, contents include: introduction, project developer background, the concept, market analysis, leasing strategy, financing, offer to purchase, and exhibits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents correspondence, speeches, flyers for community events, newsletters and essays not explicitly created by the ABMPTP but created by community within the same context, and in many cases addressing systemic inequity of the entertainment industry.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication with various production companies, multiple addressed to Mr. Alfred Foster of Kamal Production.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essays on the African American/Black and minority experience in Hollywood, including a paper titled: "Conflict Between Minorities and the Media." One special review issue of Chamba Notes, a Pan-African oriented quarterly newsletter that deals with all film activity.

did.unittitle: General: Event Brochures, Flyers, and Handouts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple brochures including from: the Black Cinema Expo and a Gala Cocktail Dance Saluting Mayor Doris A. Davis, and the First Annual Windwalker Awards Benefit. As well as some communication about events and handouts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Two original folders maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various film proposals, including: 'What Does It Take', a separate proposal by Toka Films, and proposals by Black Cinema inc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Two original folders maintained in original order, loose papers arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records from the Black Stuntmen, Stuntwomen's Association breaking down yearly earnings from 1975. Pamphlet titled, Behind the Scenes: Equal Employment Opportunity in the Motion Picture Industry. The Capital Press Club Membership Roster from 1980-1981. The green folder contains names and contact information for various Black writers, Black and white Actors, and Black producers. The black folder contains information about the Black Filmmaker Foundation and the American Film Institute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Range of records including: an evaluation agreement, rating of a screening, and lists of sponsors and advertising agencies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press from the Chicago Reporter, a monthly information service on racial issues in metropolitan Chicago, the Los Angeles Times, and others. Headlines/Articles include: "Black Actors' Struggle for Equality in Films," "9 Directors Rising from the Trashes," "Black in Hollywood: Playing the Role No More," "Black Actors' Struggle for Equality in Films Has Been On-Going Since 'The Birth of a Nation.'"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Includes one original folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One speech delivered before the Afro-American Cultural Association in Los Angeles, titled: The MYTH of Universal Miscegenation. One folder containing a handwritten draft and typed draft with notes for a speech about Collette Wood.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files and sub-series organized alphabetically, one sub-series: NAACP records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents claims, events, communications, elections, hearings, and legal actions taken by the LA Human Relations Commission and the Beverly Hills/Hollywood Branch of the NAACP. While materials were not created by the ABMPTP there is overlapping interest, especially in regards to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, complaint forms filed to the NAACP against production companies, and the NAACP's annual Image Awards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents arranged chronologically. Contains one folder in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Affirmative action/employment subcommittee report, notes, and suggestions. Memos from the board of human relations commissioners. Overview of the Los Angeles human relations commission hearing on the employment exclusion of ethnic minorities in the motion picture and television industries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of folder arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission's chairmen.

did.unittitle: NAACP: Beverly Hills/Hollywood Branch Communication with General/Executive Members

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Internal communication with branch members, board members, including information about upcoming meetings, and a ballot for the 16th annual NAACP Image awards.

did.unittitle: NAACP: Beverly Hills/Hollywood Branch Financial Records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beginning balance and disbursements for June and July, 1983.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and reports from the Freedom Fund Committee, Executive Committee, and Personnel Committee. Includes a proposed calendar of events from the Freedom Fund Committee, featuring proposed programs to generate funds.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NAACP Complaint forms detailing date of incident, name of person and/or company involved, and description of incident along with any witnesses. Most forms are partially filled out, with a few completely blank. Occupation of filee's range from: actor/actress, stuntman/woman, singer, dancer. Grievances filed against 20th Century Fox, Universal Studios, and Paramount.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer for African Liberation Day put on Councilman Robert Farrell of district 8 and the Kawaida Groundwork Committee, information about the NAACP's National Black Dollar Days organized to "impress local and national businesses-as well as the Black community with the importance of the Black spending power in the U.S."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents maintained in original order within an original folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beverly Hills/Hollywood Branch Treasurer's report and executive meeting minutes from early 1979. Communication with members from 1973, and an undated list of branch members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two brochures from the 14th and 16th Annual NAACP Image Awards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains brochures from the 31st and 32nd NAACP Image Awards, some communication regarding ticket information and nominations, handwritten notes from the NAACP Labor and Industry Committee.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials contained within an original folder maintained in original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15th and 16th annual NAACP Image Awards financial reports, accompanied with notes from an executive board meeting of the Beverly Hills/Hollywood Branch. Image Awards financial report from August and September 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minority Telecommunications Development Program, communication from the Congressional Black Caucus Communications Braintrust, an agenda from the Minority Entrepreneurship in communications: Broadcast, Cable, New Technologies and Program Production.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: an official manual on branch election procedure, communication to members and meeting minutes regarding a challenge to election results, copies of a hearing before the national NAACP office brought forth by Lillian Cumber alleging 'illegal and unlawful election procedures.'Several unfilled ballots from the disputed 1980 election, signatures of candidates, some treasurer's reports, a draft from the National Black Media Coalition, West Coast region NAACP Branch Election Report of officers and executive committee for 1965-1966.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Maintained in original order within a folder originally labeled 'NAACP Nominations Committee'.
